paranets.purpose: Real-time human+agent implementation coordination and delivery state
Trust, provenance, and adversarial validation experiments

coordinationModel.goal: Autonomous multi-agent coordination with human oversight, without central manual orchestration

workstreams.name: Collaboration Substrate V1
workstreams.summary: Operationalize DKG-native coordination for humans and agents in shared production paranet.
workstreams.description: Build claim/progress/handoff protocols, runbook automations, and graph query packs so teams coordinate from graph state.
workstreams.roiHypothesis: Reduce coordination overhead by 30% and improve throughput by 25% in multi-agent development.

workstreams.name: Speed-Cost Frontier Optimization
workstreams.summary: Tune model mix + collaboration protocol for best throughput-per-dollar.
workstreams.description: Run structured experiments across model sizes and collaboration modes to identify production default strategy.
workstreams.roiHypothesis: Improve features-per-dollar by 20% while keeping completion >=95%.

workstreams.name: Trust, Provenance, and Auditability
workstreams.summary: Make graph artifacts suitable for compliance-grade reviews and adversarial conditions.
workstreams.description: Add trust metrics, contradiction tracing, and machine-verifiable provenance for decisions and evidence.
workstreams.roiHypothesis: Cut incident root-cause time by 40% and reduce rollback risk through stronger decision traceability.

workstreams.name: Production Hardening
workstreams.summary: Close persistence, async task, and reliability gaps needed for autonomous operation.
workstreams.description: Implement crash recovery, async long-running skills, key management hardening, and private data redundancy strategy.
workstreams.roiHypothesis: Reduce outage and recovery cost by reducing failure-induced rework and downtime.

tasks.name: Graph-native claim/progress/handoff protocol
tasks.description: Define and enforce standard event entities for claim, progress, decision, handoff, completion in dkgv9-production paranet.

tasks.name: Shared paranet bootstrap and runbook
tasks.description: Automate creation/health checks/subscription for dkgv9-production and dkgv9-trust-lab paranets across human+agent teams.

tasks.name: Smaller model with graph assistance evaluation
tasks.description: Run controlled comparisons of smaller vs larger models under Control, SPARQL, and C2 collaboration to identify best ROI frontier.

tasks.name: Trust metrics extraction pipeline
tasks.description: Implement automated trust scoring over graph events: provenance completeness, conflict traceability, audit reconstruction.

experiments.name: ROI Experiment: smaller models + graph collaboration
experiments.summary: Test if smaller models become more competitive when collaboration knowledge is externalized in the DKG.
experiments.hypothesis: Smaller models + C2 can match or exceed larger-model control on cost-per-success while maintaining >=95% completion.

experiments.name: Swarm on same task: shared Markdown vs DKG graph
experiments.summary: Evaluate swarm coordination quality when all agents work on one interdependent task.
experiments.hypothesis: DKG coordination reduces conflict and rework at equal or lower cost than markdown coordination.

experiments.name: Trust-first experiment: provenance and auditability
experiments.summary: Compare trust properties of md-based collaboration vs DKG-based collaboration under conflicting requirements.
experiments.hypothesis: DKG produces significantly higher provenance and audit trace quality even when speed/cost are similar.

initialDecisions.summary: Production collaboration state should be DKG-native, with Markdown as optional human-readable projection.
initialDecisions.rationale: Experiment C showed collaboration substrate changes reliability and traceability. We need machine-queryable shared state for autonomous coordination.

initialDecisions.summary: Default multi-agent execution should use shared paranet protocols with claim/progress/handoff events.
initialDecisions.rationale: Parallel runs improve speed; structured shared state is required to avoid drift and central coordinator bottlenecks.
